title: TheWatcher — Cross-Platform System Monitoring Platform
description: C++20 agent/server platform using ZeroMQ + libsodium for secure transport, MessagePack for wire serialisation, SQLite/PostgreSQL for storage, and a Node.js dashboard. Agents collect CPU, memory, disk, temperature, process, and network metrics and ship them to the server every 30 s. The server exposes a REST API consumed by a Node.js process that pushes updates to browsers over Socket.io.
acceptance_criteria: Agent compiles and runs on Linux, Windows, and FreeBSD with Bazel
Server compiles and runs on the same three platforms
Agent completes dynamic enrollment and connects to server with CURVE encryption
Server rejects unapproved agents via ZAP
Agent sends SystemMetrics every 30 s; server stores to SQLite
All predefined commands (SET_INTERVAL, SET_PROCESS_LIMIT, RESTART_COLLECTORS, PAUSE, RESUME, GET_STATUS, DISCONNECT) are handled
REST API returns agents list, latest metrics, and metrics history
Node.js dashboard shows live data per agent in browser

tasks.title: Bazel build system
tasks.description: MODULE.bazel, WORKSPACE.bazel, .bazelrc, root BUILD.bazel, and all third_party BUILD stubs for libzmq, libsodium, cppzmq, msgpack-cxx, cpp-httplib, sqlite3.
acceptance_criteria: bazel build //agent:agent succeeds on Linux
bazel build //server:server succeeds on Linux
All external deps declared with urls and build_file pointers
tasks.implementation_notes: libzmq: cmake via rules_foreign_cc. libsodium: configure_make (Unix) — Windows requires MSYS2, documented in .bazelrc.example. cppzmq, msgpack-cxx, cpp-httplib: header-only cc_library. sqlite3: single-file amalgamation cc_library.

tasks.title: Common library
tasks.description: Shared headers: metrics.hpp (structs + MSGPACK_DEFINE_ARRAY + nlohmann to_json/from_json), protocol.hpp (Frame type, encode/decode helpers), commands.hpp (CommandType enum + payload structs), crypto.hpp (CURVE keypair gen via libsodium).
acceptance_criteria: SystemMetrics round-trips through msgpack without data loss
Frame encode/decode is symmetric
nlohmann serialisation produces valid JSON for all metric types
tasks.implementation_notes: Files: common/metrics.hpp, common/protocol.hpp, common/commands.hpp, common/crypto.hpp. Use MSGPACK_DEFINE_ARRAY (positional, order fixed). nlohmann ADL: NLOHMANN_DEFINE_TYPE_NON_INTRUSIVE for each struct. crypto.hpp: zmq_curve_keypair wraps libsodium Curve25519.

tasks.title: Agent
tasks.description: C++20 executable that collects system metrics and ships them to the server.
acceptance_criteria: Agent enrolls, receives approval, then connects with CURVE
Metrics sent every interval_seconds (default 30)
All seven commands handled correctly
Config persisted to agent.json between restarts

subtasks.title: Collector interface + all six collectors
subtasks.description: Abstract Collector base (collector.hpp). Concrete: cpu, memory, disk, temperature (Linux/BSD stub, Windows skipped), process (top-N configurable), network. Each uses platform ifdefs: Linux=/proc, Windows=Win32 API, BSD=sysctl.
acceptance_criteria: Each collector compiles on all three platforms
CpuCollector uses two-sample delta for utilisation
ProcessCollector returns top-N by CPU, N configurable at runtime
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: agent/collectors/collector.hpp + {cpu,memory,disk,temperature,process,network}_collector.{hpp,cpp}. Linux CPU: /proc/stat two-sample. Windows CPU: GetSystemTimes delta. BSD CPU: kern.cp_time sysctl. Disk I/O: /proc/diskstats (Linux), GetDiskFreeSpaceEx + no I/O rate (Windows), statfs (BSD).

subtasks.title: Enrollment client
subtasks.description: agent/enrollment.hpp+cpp. REQ socket to port 5556 (plain, no CURVE). Sends EnrollRequest msgpack frame. Polls until EnrollResponse.approved == true. On approval, disconnects and signals agent to connect to main port.
acceptance_criteria: Enrollment request reaches server and is stored as pending
Repeated polling with configurable backoff
Returns on approval or throws on rejection
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: agent/enrollment.hpp, agent/enrollment.cpp. Uses zmq::socket_t(ctx, ZMQ_REQ). Sends Frame{ENROLL_REQUEST, agent_id, ts, pack(EnrollRequest{agent_id, hostname, platform, curve_public_key_z85})}. Polls every 10 s.

subtasks.title: Agent core + config + main
subtasks.description: agent/config.hpp+cpp (load/save agent.json, generate keypair on first run). agent/agent.hpp+cpp (DEALER socket, jthreads for collection, command listening, heartbeat). agent/main.cpp (CLI flags, config path).
acceptance_criteria: Config auto-generated with new UUID and fresh keypair on first run
Agent reconnects on server restart
All commands mutate live state without restart
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: agent/config.hpp, agent/config.cpp, agent/agent.hpp, agent/agent.cpp, agent/main.cpp. Use std::jthread + std::stop_token for all background loops. ZMQ identity set to agent_id via zmq::sockopt::routing_id. Heartbeat every 10 s.

tasks.title: Server
tasks.description: C++20 executable: ZMQ ROUTER (port 5555, CURVE), ZAP handler for per-agent auth, enrollment REP (port 5556, plain), SQLite store, REST API (cpp-httplib, port 8080).
acceptance_criteria: Unapproved agents rejected at ZAP level
Metrics stored and queryable via REST
Commands dispatched to correct agent by ZMQ identity
PostgreSQL stub compiles and throws 'not implemented'

subtasks.title: Database abstraction + SQLite + PostgreSQL stub
subtasks.description: server/store.hpp (pure virtual Store interface). server/store_sqlite.hpp+cpp (SQLite3 C API, two tables: agents + metrics). server/store_postgres.hpp+cpp (stub, same interface, throws not_implemented).
acceptance_criteria: Schema created on first run: agents (id, hostname, platform, curve_public_key, status, config_*) + metrics (agent_id, collected_at, metrics_json)
get_latest_metrics() returns most recent row per agent
get_metrics_range() supports since/until/limit filters
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: server/store.hpp, server/store_sqlite.{hpp,cpp}, server/store_postgres.{hpp,cpp}. SQLite opened with WAL mode. Metrics stored as JSON TEXT (decoded from msgpack in server). Index on (agent_id, collected_at). Retention enforced by pruning rows older than config.retention_days on startup.

subtasks.title: ZAP handler
subtasks.description: server/zap_handler.hpp+cpp. Binds inproc://zeromq.zap.01. Runs in std::jthread. Checks connecting agent's 32-byte CURVE public key (z85-encoded) against approved_keys set. Allows dynamic key add/remove without restart.
acceptance_criteria: 200 response for keys in approved set, 400 otherwise
Thread-safe key add/remove via mutex
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: server/zap_handler.hpp, server/zap_handler.cpp. ZAP request frame[6] = 32-byte raw public key. zmq_z85_encode to convert to string for lookup. std::shared_mutex for readers/writers. Methods: add_key(z85), remove_key(z85), has_key(z85).

subtasks.title: REST API
subtasks.description: server/api.hpp+cpp. cpp-httplib server on port 8080. All responses JSON via nlohmann. Endpoints: GET /api/v1/agents, GET /api/v1/agents/:id, POST /approve/:id, POST /reject/:id, GET /api/v1/agents/:id/metrics, GET /api/v1/agents/:id/latest, POST /api/v1/agents/:id/command, PUT /api/v1/agents/:id/config, GET /api/v1/summary.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints return correct HTTP status codes
Command endpoint enqueues command for ZMQ dispatch thread
Config PUT updates DB and sends CONFIG_UPDATE frame to agent
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: server/api.hpp, server/api.cpp. Shared Store* and command queue (std::queue + std::mutex + std::condition_variable) with Server. Command queue used by ZMQ dispatch thread. cpp-httplib runs in its own thread pool.

subtasks.title: Server core + main
subtasks.description: server/server.hpp+cpp: starts all components (ZAP, ROUTER, enrollment REP, REST API), runs ZMQ poll loop. server/config.hpp+cpp: load server.json. server/main.cpp: CLI.
acceptance_criteria: Clean shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
All threads joined on stop()
subtasks.implementation_notes: Files: server/server.hpp, server/server.cpp, server/config.hpp, server/config.cpp, server/main.cpp. ZMQ poll loop uses zmq_poll on [router, enrollment_rep] with 100ms timeout to check stop token. Command queue drained after each poll tick.

tasks.title: Node.js dashboard
tasks.description: Express server + Socket.io backend. Polls C++ REST API every 5 s, pushes updates to browsers. Browser: plain HTML + Chart.js + Socket.io client.
acceptance_criteria: npm start launches dashboard on port 3000
Agent list shown with status indicators
CPU/memory gauges update in real time
Process table shows top-N
Historical CPU/memory chart (last hour)
tasks.implementation_notes: Files: dashboard/package.json, dashboard/server.js, dashboard/public/{index.html,style.css,app.js}. Uses axios for HTTP polling. Socket.io emits 'metrics' event with full summary payload. Chart.js for line charts.
